rulings.Case Title: CHARLES NAYLOR JR VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, July 15, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Tosco Refining Company¿s motion for summary adjudication is denied. The court may not grant summary adjudication as to an issue of damages unless it disposes of an entire cause of action or is to punitive damages. (DeCastro West Chadorow & Burns, Inc. v. Superior Court (1996) 47 Cal.App.4th 410; CCP s 437c(f)(1)). A motion seeking to exclude only non-economic damages does not dispose of an entire cause of action because a plaintiff might still obtain economic damages. In all events, defendant failed to satidfy its intial burden of production since it has not shown that plaintiff does not possess and could not reasonably obtain evidence from persons other than Mr. Naylor to prove non-economic damages. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JAY WARREN CALHOUN et al VS. ASBESTO DEFENDANTS B*P AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, July 15, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 7. Defendant Union Carbide Corporation¿s motion for summary adjudication is granted in part and denied in part. The motion is denied as to issues 1 and 2 because, assuming without deciding that California will adopt a ¿sophisticated intermediary defense,¿ the most likely articulation of that defense to be adopted by California¿s appellate courts is the ¿Restatement approach¿ that is apparently followed by the majority of the courts to address the issue and is set forth in among other cases in Willis v. Raymark Industries, Inc. (4th Cir. 1990) 905 F.2d 793. (See 74 Virginia Law Review 579.) Defendant has not shown that it has met all the requirements of the ¿Restatement approach.¿ The motion is granted as to issues 3 and 4 based on plaintiffs¿ representation that the cause of action for false representation and claim for punitive damages will be dismissed, although it appears that the dismissal has not been filed. =(220/HEK)
